arts.ttl: Predicted Hull City XI to face Watford as Ruben Selles ponders attacking changes
arts.sum: While Joao Pedro's return after a three-game suspension was not a surprise, Selles' decision to hand John Egan a first start since February at the expense of Alfie Jones was, and perhaps, for some, so was Regan Slater's recall at the expense of Steven Alzate. City's predicted starting XI: Pandur, McLoughlin, Hughes, Egan, Coyle (c), Alzate, Slater, Gelhardt, Crooks, Kamara, Pedro.

arts.ttl: Hull City boss addresses future of Tigers man amid Derby County and Stoke City transfer links
arts.sum: Ruben Selles has praised the attitude of Hull City midfielder Regan Slater amid growing interest in the former Sheffield United youngster. Given Slater was born a Blade and came in for some typical post-game comments from Wednesday fans earlier in the season after successive MKM Stadium defeats, playing in that win would have meant something a little bit extra.

arts.ttl: Hull City's survival hopes assessed after dramatic Sheffield Wednesday late show
arts.sum: They believe City will finish 18th by the time the full-time whistle blows at Fratton Park on the final afternoon with 52 points - a 4.7 per cent of going down - five ahead of Cardiff City, who are backed to go down to League One along with Luton Town and Plymouth Argyle. City are backed to finish above both Stoke City and Oxford United, and level on points with their final day opponents Portsmouth along with QPR.

arts.ttl: Hull City boss Ruben Selles keen to keep building connections after Sheffield Wednesday triumph
arts.sum: Hull City head coach Ruben Selles believes moments like Charlie Hughes' thumping winning goal at Sheffield Wednesday on Saturday helps create a connection between the team and the supporters. You want to be a professional for those moments in the game, the moments when you score that goal, to win it at the end, that means something for the club in front of your fans.

arts.ttl: Sheffield Wednesday boss Danny Rohl highlights two Hull City heroes in Owls defeat
arts.sum: City goalkeeper Ivor Pandur didn't have a huge amount to do with Wednesday, only mustering four efforts on target in the game, but two of them were especially good with the Croatian denying Josh Windass early in the second half and then Dominic Iorfa after Hughes' powerful header, but his performance caught the eye of the beleaguered German, who was impressed by his own side's showing against a backdrop of more off-field chaos. Sheffield Wednesday chief Danny Rohl admits he was impressed by Charlie Hughes' winning goal and the performance of goalkeeper Ivor Pandur.

arts.ttl: Matchwinner Charlie Hughes picks out key Hull City performer as he targets Watford success
arts.sum: Charlie Hughes was full of praise for goalkeeper Ivor Pandur after the Hull City goalkeeper played his part in helping the Tigers claim a huge 1-0 win at Sheffield Wednesday. Attention for City now turns to Tuesday night's trip to Watford, and another chance for the Tigers to win back-to-back games for just the second time this season, and really back up this result, something Hughes is desperate to see his side do.
